definitions.definition: The atomic number of an element is the number of protons present in the nucleus of an atom of that element. It is a unique identifier for each element and determines its position in the periodic table. The atomic number of hydrogen is 1.

Isotopes are atoms of the same element that have the same number of protons but differ in the number of neutrons. Hydrogen has three naturally occurring isotopes: protium (1 proton, 0 neutrons), deuterium (1 proton, 1 neutron), and tritium (1 proton, 2 neutrons).
The electronic configuration of an atom is the arrangement of electrons in its orbitals. The electronic configuration of hydrogen is 1s1, indicating that the single electron is in the 1s orbital.
The atomic mass of an element is the average mass of the naturally occurring isotopes. The atomic mass of hydrogen is approximately 1.00794 u.
Valency is the number of electrons an atom can gain, lose, or share to form a chemical bond. Hydrogen has a valency of 1, meaning it can form one bond with other atoms.
A reducing agent is a substance that donates electrons to another substance, causing it to be reduced. Hydrogen is a reducing agent because it can donate electrons to form hydrogen ions.
An oxidizing agent is a substance that accepts electrons from another substance, causing it to be oxidized. Hydrogen is an oxidizing agent because it can accept electrons to form hydrogen ions.

A hydrogen bond is a type of intermolecular force that arises between molecules with a hydrogen atom bonded to a highly electronegative atom (such as oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine). Hydrogen bonds play a crucial role in the structure and properties of water.
The boiling point of a substance is the temperature at which its vapor pressure equals the surrounding pressure. The boiling point of hydrogen is -252.88°C.
The melting point of a substance is the temperature at which its solid and liquid phases coexist in equilibrium. The melting point of hydrogen is -259.11°C.
The density of a substance is its mass per unit volume. The density of hydrogen is approximately 0.0899 g/L at standard temperature and pressure.

Physical properties are characteristics of a substance that can be observed or measured without changing its chemical composition. Hydrogen has several physical properties, including its boiling point, melting point, and density.

Chemical properties are characteristics of a substance that can be observed or measured when it undergoes a chemical reaction. Hydrogen has several chemical properties, including its reactivity with other elements and compounds.

A combustion reaction is a chemical reaction in which a substance reacts with oxygen to produce heat and light. Hydrogen can undergo a combustion reaction with oxygen to form water and release energy.
A synthesis reaction is a chemical reaction in which two or more substances combine to form a new substance. Hydrogen can undergo a synthesis reaction with oxygen to form water.
Electrolysis is the process of using an electric current to drive a chemical reaction. Hydrogen can be produced through electrolysis of water.
Hydrogen peroxide is a compound that consists of two hydrogen atoms and two oxygen atoms (H2O2). It is a strong oxidizing agent and is commonly used as a disinfectant.
Hydrogen chloride is a compound that consists of one hydrogen atom and one chlorine atom (HCl). It is a strong acid and is commonly used as a chemical reagent.
